summary: FAIL — Coder fixed FluidProvider bare catch and TryResolvePath, but 3 issues remain: (1) 6 bare catch{} blocks elsewhere in Runtime2 not addressed, (2) TryResolvePath new catch has no fatal exception filter, (3) callGoal test assertion still only checks static text not dynamic value.

previous_reviews_assessed.codeanalyzer: partial — missed the bare catch{} pattern across the codebase, not just FluidProvider.
previous_reviews_assessed.tester: agree — but callGoal assertion fix is still weak.
previous_reviews_assessed.security: agree — MaxSteps/MaxRecursion addition is good defense-in-depth.

findings.issue: 6 bare catch{} blocks remain in Runtime2. Coder only fixed FluidProvider but did not scan the codebase as requested. Locations: TypeMapping.cs:312, TypeMapping.cs:352, SignedData.cs:60, DefaultHttpProvider.cs:485, DefaultHttpProvider.cs:615, DefaultHttpProvider.cs:653. All silently swallow exceptions including fatal ones.
findings.impact: Same pattern Ingi escalated — silent error swallowing masks bugs. Each needs at minimum a fatal exception filter.
findings.suggestion: Add catch filters to all 6: catch (Exception ex) when (ex is not (NullReferenceException or OutOfMemoryException or StackOverflowException)). For the ones that return a fallback value, that's fine — just don't catch fatal exceptions.

findings.issue: callGoal success test assertion Contains('Number:') only checks static template text, not the dynamic goal result. Template is 'Number: {% callGoal GetNumber %}' — 'Number:' is always present regardless of what callGoal produces. Should assert Contains('42') to verify the goal's value appears.
findings.impact: A bug in callGoal value serialization (e.g., ToString() returning type name instead of value) would still pass this test.

findings.issue: New TryResolvePath catch (Exception ex) has no filter — catches OOM, NRE, SOE. Inconsistent with the fatal exception filters at lines 48 and 104 in the same file.
findings.impact: Low — TryResolvePath returns null which is a safe fallback, but fatal exceptions should never be caught.
findings.suggestion: Add filter: catch (Exception ex) when (ex is not (NullReferenceException or OutOfMemoryException or StackOverflowException))

findings.issue: Former bare catch{} in RegisterTypeIfNeeded — removed entirely. Exceptions now propagate to outer catch filter.
TryResolvePath now catches ValidatePath exceptions and returns null — user sees 'file not found' instead of generic render error.
